prompt:
Our library's summer reading kiosk wants hangman, and the display team already carved the gallows into the UI spec, so the art is non-negotiable. Word lists are curated by the librarians as plain files — one answer per line, lowercase letters, phrases allowed with single spaces. Build hangman.ts (stdlib only, no npm) against the shipped fixtures; test_hangman.ts and words.txt are read-only.

loadWords(text) parses a list: blank lines are skipped, and every entry must match lowercase words separated by single spaces — no capitals, no doubled or leading/trailing spaces, no punctuation; bad entries throw ('bad word list entry'). An empty list throws too. newGame(answer) applies the same validation ('bad answer') and returns plain data: { answer, guessed, misses, status } with guessed the letters in the order tried, and status 'playing' | 'won' | 'lost'. Export MAX_MISSES = 6.

guess(game, letter) is pure — never mutates, returns a new state. The letter must be a single a-z character ('guess must be a single lowercase letter...'), and guessing on a finished game throws ('game is over'). House rule, pinned: a REPEATED guess costs nothing and changes nothing — return the EXACT SAME object, misses untouched, guessed not re-appended. A hit reveals every occurrence of the letter; the game is won when all letters are revealed (spaces are free). A miss bumps misses; the sixth miss loses.

mask(game) renders the puzzle: guessed letters show, spaces show as spaces from the very start (phrase mode), everything else is '_'. gallows(game) returns the ladder stage for the current miss count, exactly these seven (joined with \n, no trailing newline):

stage 0:
+---+
|   |
|
|
|
=====

stage 1 adds the head (line 3 becomes '|   o'); stage 2 the body (line 4 '|   |'); stage 3 one arm (line 4 '|  /|'); stage 4 both arms (line 4 '|  /|\'); stage 5 one leg (line 5 '|  /'); stage 6 both legs (line 5 '|  / \'). Every other line is as stage 0.

transcript(answer, guesses) replays a guess string one letter at a time: a hit logs 'guess x: hit <mask>', a miss 'guess x: miss <n>/6 <mask>', a repeat 'guess x: repeat <mask>'; the moment the game ends append 'won <answer>' or 'lost <answer>' and ignore any remaining letters. A script may stop mid-game with no closing line. Lines joined with \n.

node --test test_hangman.ts must pass as shipped.
